description: Crews erected scaffolding on Friday as onlookers gathered into the evening, though storms delayed the work until Saturday.

description: Protesters gather at Belfast City Hall for a demonstration against racism, after the past week of violence and disorder.

description: Derbyshire Police say the officer has been removed from frontline duties while it investigates.

description: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5, released publicly this week, sparked concerns about cybersecurity and hacking.

description: Essex Police say three teenagers are being questioned on suspicion of murder.

description: England recover most of the training equipment that was stolen prior to their arrival in Kansas City on Saturday.

description: Eileen Fox had worked at the One Stop Shop in Bootle for four years before she was sacked.

description: The fleece came from a Lincoln Longwool sheep on a farm near Spilsby.

description: At his lowest ebb Edward Bartlett was playing video games 20 hours a day. Now he goes birdwatching.

description: Granny H has been leaving hundreds of knitted teddy bears, rabbits, dogs and lambs in Hartford village.

description: Art teacher Helene Plotkin bought the work in White Plans, New York in 1966, unaware it was by Scottish Colourist FCB Cadell.

title: PM Modi to hold bilateral talks with Trump at G7 Summit; first meeting in 16 months
Humiliation by colleagues, harassment by 'online friend': Pune TCS techie suicide case gets murkier
description: A 48-year-old TCS employee in Pune died by suicide, allegedly due to workplace harassment, difficulties with leave after a family bereavement, and stress from his wife's chronic illness. A suicide note named two female colleagues and a man, leading to an abetment to suicide case registered by police.

title: 'All vessels should comply with orders from US forces in Hormuz', Rubio tells Jaishankar
Canada's sixth-largest state Manitoba retires student pathway to permanent residency
description: Manitoba has closed its Career Employment Pathway for international graduates, effective immediately. Candidates with active EOIs and six months of provincial work experience can now be considered under the Skilled Worker in Manitoba Pathway. This change aims to align graduates' skills with labor market needs, prioritizing those who studied and are working in the province.

title: Watch: SriLankan Airlines flight with over 200 onboard hit by lightning; lands safely
description: A SriLankan Airlines flight bound for Sydney was forced to return to Colombo on Friday after being struck by lightning. Passengers reported hearing a loud bang and seeing sparks from an engine. The crew managed the situation, and the aircraft landed safely back in Colombo due to minor engine damage.

title: Mamata top aide Sudip Bandyopadhyay meets BJP leadership in Delhi: Is he 20th rebel MP?
Rohit Sharma surpasses Mohinder Amarnath, becomes oldest male cricketer to ...
description: At 39 years and 75 days, he went past former India all-rounder Mohinder Amarnath, who was 39 years and 36 days old when he played his last ODI for India in 1989.

title: US ban on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 has an Amazon 'link'
description: The US government has ordered AI firm Anthropic to halt access to two of its models. This decision stems from national security concerns. Anthropic disagrees, citing a limited jailbreak technique. Amazon researchers reportedly demonstrated this technique. The company states the identified vulnerabilities were minor and already known. The shutdown affects users worldwide.

title: Darnell Nurse trade request leaves Edmonton Oilers facing difficult NHL offseason decisions ahead
description: The Edmonton Oilers face a difficult challenge heading into the NHL offseason, with Darnell Nurse reportedly looking for a fresh start. There's been some trade speculation, but the veteran defenseman's large contract could scare off suitors. It would take a lot of salary gymnastics to make any deal happen, which is why it is one of the most closely followed NHL updates.

title: Bullet train project: India installs tunnel hoods - how they keep your journey smooth
description: India's ambitious Mumbai-Ahmedabad Bullet Train project is introducing tunnel hood technology for the first time, enhancing high-speed rail travel. This innovation manages air pressure changes in mountain tunnels, significantly reducing noise and ensuring smoother, quieter journeys for passengers and surrounding communities. The technology employs pressure-relief vents to gradually equalize air, mirroring global best practices for advanced rail infrastructure.

title: Forced to work 16 hrs daily, abused: West Bengal woman rescued from Gurugram flat
description: In a shocking incident, a woman from Birbhum, West Bengal, was freed from a high-security apartment in Gurugram after being reportedly trapped for more than two years under the horrific conditions of forced domestic servitude. Each day, she toiled for 16 hours amidst appalling abuse, her escape thwarted by stringent security measures.

title: Bill Gates isn't happy with US govt taking stake in Intel, IBM & other US companies
description: Bill Gates has raised concerns over the Trump administration taking equity stakes in Intel, IBM and other US companies, saying that the "rules of the game are pretty unclear right now." The Microsoft co-founder warned Washington could favour firms it owns over rivals with better technology. His comments come as Trump plans to meet AI executives from OpenAI, Anthropic and others to discuss similar government stakes.

title: Outstanding! Gill takes one-handed blinder in 1st ODI - Watch
description: Shubman Gill stole the spotlight with a spectacular one-handed diving catch as India tightened control in the rain-reduced first ODI against Afghanistan in Dharamshala. Afghanistan posted a strong start through Rahmanullah Gurbaz’s quick century, but India struck back with regular wickets. Debutant Harsh Dubey impressed with key breakthroughs, while Arshdeep Singh and Nitish Kumar Reddy supported well to restrict the visitors.

title: 48-ball mayhem! Gurbaz rewrites history with ton vs India
description: Rahmanullah Gurbaz smashed a record-breaking 48-ball century against India in the rain-affected first ODI. His explosive 102 off 51 balls, featuring eight fours and eight sixes, marked the fastest ODI hundred by an Afghanistan batter and the quickest in an India-Afghanistan encounter. Gurbaz's rapid fifty also secured a spot in Afghanistan's top ODI half-centuries.

title: INDIA bloc hits reset button: Why 5-point truce plan of opposition alliance may not work
description: The INDIA bloc attempted a reset with a five-point plan to counter the BJP, but internal state-level rivalries remain a significant hurdle. Despite national unity efforts, allies like Congress and Samajwadi Party, or Congress and AAP, clash in state elections, undermining the coalition's strength and relevance. Addressing these deep-seated conflicts is crucial for lasting opposition unity.

title: 'RSS most misunderstood': Mohan Bhagwat explains best way to understand Sangh
description: Addressing the RSS centenary celebrations, chief Mohan Bhagwat declared the organization the world's largest voluntary body, yet "most misunderstood." He explained the outreach aims to clarify misconceptions, noting external views range from paramilitary to gymnasium. Bhagwat emphasized direct engagement as the best way to understand the Sangh's true nature and its century-long mission of national welfare.

title: Sudarshan Chakra: India develops multi-layered air shield to stop missiles, drones & rockets
description: India is bolstering its air defence with the "Sudarshan Chakra" project, a multi-layered shield against evolving aerial threats. Recent successful trials of its Ballistic Missile Defence system and operational successes highlight India's commitment to intercepting missiles and drones, safeguarding national security against regional adversaries.

title: When 'Pirates of the Caribbean' actor Johnny Depp explained the beauty of emotions
description: Johnny Depp's Hollywood comeback is gaining momentum with upcoming films like 'Ebenezer: A Christmas Carol' and 'Day Drinker.' A resurfaced 1995 interview quote about love and anger, once a personal reflection, gained significant courtroom attention during his defamation trial.  This highlights a complex personal narrative now intertwined with his professional resurgence.

title: 'Baseless': TMC shoots down Congress merger speculations, calls reports ‘fake news’
'Masked woman enters, exits building': CCTV clue deepens mystery in Delhi Govindpuri fire case
description: A CCTV clip shows a masked woman entering a residential building shortly before a deadly fire broke out, killing three family members. Investigators are analyzing her movements for any connection to the blaze, which initially suggested a short circuit. The footage captures her entering and exiting the premises before flames became visible.

title: Akbar, Genghis Khan and ironically Stalin: 8 people richer than Elon Musk
description: When we marvel at Elon Musk's astonishing trillion-dollar net worth, largely bolstered by SpaceX's remarkable achievements, it’s essential to draw parallels with history's most influential monarchs. Leaders like Mansa Musa, Genghis Khan, and Akbar the Great presided over vast empires whose economic legacies, when adjusted for inflation, dwarf Musk’s wealth.

title: Middle East peace 24 hrs away? Pak PM suggests US, Iran may finalise deal soon
description: Pakistan's PM Shehbaz Sharif hinted that a peace deal between the US and Iran could be finalized within 24 hours, marking a significant diplomatic breakthrough. Both nations have indicated progress, with an agreed-upon text reportedly reached. US officials, however, urge caution against speculation, emphasizing that economic benefits for Iran are contingent on fulfilling commitments.

title: 'You win a Test & can't celebrate?': Ex-England player blasts ECB amid Stokes row
description: Stokes came under scrutiny after footage emerged from post-Test celebrations at a nightclub following England's win over New Zealand at Lord's. Stokes and teammate Gus Atkinson were involved in an incident that also included Saracens academy rugby player Totoa Auvaa.

title: Found in Epstein files: Clue about missing German model surfaces after 11 years - report
description: A German model, Michele, missing for 11 years, has reappeared in the Jeffrey Epstein files. Model recruiter Daniel Siad allegedly discussed her with Epstein before her 2015 disappearance. Her family fears the worst. Authorities could not launch an active search without evidence of a crime. Her fate remains unknown.

title: NFL trade rumors: Broncos, Cowboys linked to Saints star Alvin Kamara
description: NFL trade rumors around Alvin Kamara are heating up as the New Orleans Saints evaluate their offensive future ahead of the 2026 season. The veteran running back remains under contract through 2026, but growing speculation links him with potential interest from the Denver Broncos and Dallas Cowboys. Both teams are seen as strong schematic fits due to Kamara’s dual-threat ability as a runner and receiver. With Sean Payton leading Denver and Dallas seeking backfield consistency, Kamara’s name continues to circulate in league discussions as a possible late-offseason trade candidate

title: Silent, cheap & deadly: Why lasers are a gamechanger in air defence - where India stands
description: India is rapidly advancing its laser weapon capabilities, joining global efforts to counter drones and missiles. Under Project Sudarshan Chakra, DRDO is developing high-energy lasers to bolster air defence, offering a cost-effective and sustainable solution. This strategic move aims to enhance national security and achieve technological self-reliance against growing regional threats.

title: India's Afghanistan Test hero Suthar shines on Warwickshire debut
description: Manav Suthar continued his impressive form by taking two wickets on his Warwickshire debut against Yorkshire in the County Championship, days after starring on his India Test debut against Afghanistan with a seven-wicket haul. Meanwhile, veteran pacer Jaydev Unadkat shone for Sussex, claiming 4/29 to help dismiss Glamorgan for 155.

title: Bullets rain at Chandigarh store: Cashier shot dead, 13 rounds fired; CCTV footage emerges
description: Unidentified assailants opened fire at a medical store in Chandigarh's Sector 11, critically injuring cashier Janki Das, who later died. Two masked men entered the store and fired multiple rounds before fleeing with a third accomplice waiting outside on a motorcycle. Police are investigating, collecting CCTV footage, and have set up roadblocks.

title: US and Iran prepared for a ground war over uranium before truce talks drew closer: Report
Harsh Dubey, Gurnoor Brar handed maiden ODI caps as India opt to field
description: India handed ODI caps to Harsh Dubey and Gurnoor Brar for their clash against Afghanistan. Despite initial weather concerns, the match will proceed as a 25-over per side contest. Captain Shubman Gill won the toss and opted to bowl first, aiming to test new combinations ahead of the World Cup. Afghanistan, though preferring to bowl, expressed confidence in their batting.

title: Scientists have identified key ages when the body ages faster
description: New research reveals ageing is not a slow, predictable process.  Instead, the body experiences rapid biological shifts around ages 44 and 60.  These changes affect metabolism, organ function, and disease risk.  Understanding these turning points allows for proactive health measures.  Healthy habits adopted early can significantly impact well-being later in life.

title: 'Baseless': Iran lashes out after US blames Tehran of attacking ships with Indians onboard
description: Iran's embassy in India has dismissed US President Trump's accusation of a drone attack on Indian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz as "baseless." The embassy stated that Washington is attempting to divert attention from recent US strikes on three Indian-crewed ships, which resulted in the deaths of three Indian sailors.

title: Grand Congress reunification? Raut's big appeal to NCP (SP), other 'breakaway' leaders
description: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut urged former Congress allies to reunite, emphasizing the need to strengthen the party against the BJP. He dismissed claims of the Congress being a "sinking ship" and appealed to Sharad Pawar to lead the reunification efforts. Raut also criticized the BJP's historical contributions and accused Amit Shah of attempting to break opposition parties.

title: 'Male corpse private parts' joke row: MBBS student barred from entering college
description: KEM Hospital suspended MBBS student Sejal Pawar for 15 days following a controversial stand-up comedy video. An FIR has been filed against her and others for allegedly circulating objectionable content. The college cited her safety and well-being amid public backlash, recommending counseling and a detailed probe by a five-member committee.

title: 'Vijay on shopping spree of AIADMK MLAs': Stalin calls TVK govt BJP's 'xerox copy'
description: DMK president M.K. Stalin accused Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay's government of acting as a "xerox copy" of the BJP, alleging attempts to weaken the opposition by inducting AIADMK MLAs. Stalin criticized the government's approach to women's safety, claiming TVK functionaries were involved in recent crimes and that the party removed victims instead of taking action.

title: 'As soon as Rohit retires...': Sehwag reveals who will inherit India's opening slot
description: Virender Sehwag backs Yashasvi Jaiswal to become a regular in India's ODI side post-Rohit Sharma's retirement. Despite strong performances, including a century against South Africa, Jaiswal faces tough competition. Sehwag and Manoj Tiwary believe Jaiswal is poised to lead the future opening lineup once senior players depart after next year's World Cup.

title: Mouni Roy reacts to trolls after divorce: 'I'm not gay'
description: Mouni Roy has addressed persistent online rumors about her sexual orientation following her separation from Suraj Nambiar. The actress highlighted the crucial support from her female friends during this period, emphasizing the importance of emotional and financial independence for women. Roy described her life as a 'roller-coaster ride' filled with both highs and lows.

title: Brain fog is on the rise: Why more people are struggling to focus, remember, and think clearly
description: Many are experiencing increased mental fatigue, distraction, and forgetfulness, commonly known as brain fog. Experts attribute this rise to chronic stress, insufficient sleep, excessive screen time, and sedentary lifestyles. Underlying medical conditions can also contribute. Simple daily habits like adequate sleep, exercise, and stress management can help clear the fog and protect cognitive health.

title: Two Agniveervayus among 5 killed in IAF AN-32 crash in Assam
description: Five Indian Air Force personnel tragically lost their lives in an An-32 aircraft accident near Jorhat, Assam, during a routine sortie. The aircraft crashed outside the airstrip, prompting an ongoing investigation into the cause. The IAF has requested that speculation be avoided until preliminary results are available.

title: 'Played key role in modernisation': Lt Gen Dhiraj Seth to take over as next Army chief
Venezuela oil rush: US investors chase Trump's $100 billion call
description: US investment groups are actively pursuing opportunities in Venezuela's oil sector following the lifting of sanctions and a new hydrocarbons law. Lionheart Capital is leading the charge with a proposed merger to create the first Nasdaq-listed company offering direct access to Venezuelan oil assets, aiming for a $1 billion valuation.

title: How rich is a trillionaire? Musk can sponsor IPL for 17k years, spend Rs 1cr daily for 23,500 yrs
description: Elon Musk's ascent to trillionaire status, driven by Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI, redefines immense wealth. A trillion dollars, approximately Rs 95 lakh crore, dwarfs national economies and government budgets. This fortune could fund multiple Apollo missions, build numerous advanced telescopes, or purchase over 75 aircraft carriers, illustrating wealth on a scale previously unimaginable.

title: Zlatan Ibrahimovic kicks IShowSpeed off stream over Ronaldo World Cup take
description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 has already delivered plenty of memorable moments. One such incident came during Fox Sports’ coverage of the United States versus Paraguay match when former Sweden striker Zlatan Ibrahimovic shared the studio with French legend Thierry Henry and popular streamer Speed. What started as a routine discussion quickly turned into a viral social media moment that left viewers entertained.

title: Madhya Pradesh's 'Scheduled Caste Boys/Girls Housing Assistance' scheme: Eligibility, benefits and FAQs
description: Madhya Pradesh's 'Scheduled Caste Boys/Girls Housing Assistance Scheme' offers monthly rental stipends up to Rs. 2,000 for SC students studying outside their hometown. Eligibility requires MP domicile, SC status, and adherence to income limits. Applications are submitted online via the MPTAAS portal, with funds directly transferred via DBT. The scheme exclusively benefits MP residents in rented accommodation.

description: According to data from Telangana Development Planning Society as of 6 p.m., Rajendranagar recorded a rainfall of 55 mm, followed by 26.3 mm in BHEL Factory, 19.8 mm in Lingampally, 13.3 mm in Gachibowli, 11.8 mm in Chanda Nagar, 10.8 mm in University of Hyderabad and 10 mm in Patancheru
